title: [Sortie] ask_human functionality
body:
⚠️ BUILT + on `main` (2026-06-30), live-verification deferred. `sortie-ask-human.yml` (owner reply on `sortie:awaiting-human` → re-queue) + WORKFLOW.md ask/resume protocol + `sortie:awaiting-human` label all shipped. Still TODO: (a) verify the round-trip with a real Sortie run (agent can `gh` mid-turn, self-relabel survives the reconciler, re-queue resumes vs restarts); (b) Discord webhook forwarding. The max_sessions silent-park gap below is now covered by the watchdog (capped issue → `sortie:stuck` + @mention).
- NOTE (2026-06-29): when `agent.max_sessions` is exhausted, Sortie **stops dispatching but does NOT move the issue to a failed state or notify me** — it silently parks (still labeled, just never re-dispatched). Token-burn is bounded, but a stuck issue is invisible until I happen to look. **Determine the right behavior:** likely (a) transition the issue to a `sortie:failed` terminal label so it's visibly dead and drops out of candidates, AND (b) ping me on exhaustion (Discord/`ask_human`). Sortie has no auto-transition on exhaustion, so this needs a mechanism — an `after_run`/hook check on `SORTIE_ATTEMPT` vs the cap, or Sortie's `notifications` config. Ties into Discord + retry-cap items.

title: [Sortie] Mission Control / host access to the Sortie API (:7678)
body: Under the egress-hardened setup Sortie is on an `internal: true` network, so its port can't be published to the host (confirmed: no host route for internal networks). For now use `docker exec sortie curl localhost:7678/...`. When building Mission Control, run it as a container on the `egress_internal` network so it reaches `sortie:7678` container-to-container (no host port, isolation intact). Only add a forwarder sidecar (socat/nginx on both networks) if something off-NAS genuinely needs the API.

title: [Sortie] Preview branches — test a PR without checking it out locally.
body:
Spin up the app from a
given PR's code on the NAS so it can be opened in a browser on the LAN. LOE ~half day for
the single-slot manual approach below; the GUI is a follow-on.

*Constraint that shapes the design:* the NAS has no public ingress (home LAN/NAT), so cloud
CI can't reach in to deploy — same reason prod is pull-based (Watchtower polls GHCR). A
preview therefore runs **on the NAS, LAN-only** (fine — that's where I test). Cloud preview
(Fly/Railway) is rejected: it loses the `/library` mount and breaks the egress-hardened posture.

*Approach — single slot, manual (start here):*

title: [Sortie] Build half (~1–2 hrs):
body:
today `deploy.yml` builds the image only on push to `main`. Add a
PR-triggered build that pushes `ghcr.io/scolacur/personal-dashboard:pr-<n>`. **Gate to
  same-repo branches** (`if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository`)
  so untrusted fork code never builds an image — preserves deploy.yml's existing safety rule.
  Sortie branches are same-repo, so this covers the real workflow.

title: [Sortie] Run half (~2 hrs):
body:
one `preview` container, modeled on `docker/docker-compose.nas.yml`.
Parametrize the image tag (`PR_TAG=pr-17`) + a host port (e.g. **8089**, since 8080=gluetun,
  8088=prod app). Mount its **own** `/data` (a throwaway preview SQLite dir — NOT the prod
  volume) and share the DJ library `:ro` (`/volume1/music/dj-library/tracks`). An empty library
  is fine for everything except music-tracker's real-library matching. `up -d` to swap which PR
  occupies the slot.

*GUI (follow-on) — agent-dashboard "Sortie observability" section:*
- A control to **build/launch a preview from a given PR #** (button or PR picker) — sets the tag,
  recreates the preview container, surfaces the LAN URL (`http://<nas>:8089`).
- A **"currently previewing" panel**: which PR / branch / short-SHA occupies the slot right now,
  when it was launched, health, and a link to open it. Empty state when the slot is free.
- *Wiring note:* the dashboard backend (Fastify) would need to drive Docker to start/stop the
  preview container — i.e. host Docker socket access or a small NAS-side control endpoint. This
  is the same "dashboard reaches NAS infra" question as Mission Control reaching `sortie:7678`
  (see the Mission Control TODO item) and should reuse whatever mechanism that lands on. Keep the
  control on the NAS — never give a cloud surface Docker access.

*Graduate to an auto-reconciling controller (N concurrent previews, per-PR ports, optional
`pr-17.dash.lan` routing via Caddy/nginx) only if one-slot-at-a-time becomes the bottleneck —
that's ~1 day + ongoing weak-NAS RAM cost.* GUI lives in [agent-dashboard](pages/agent-dashboard/TODO.md).

title: [Sortie] 3rd exit-128 cause fixed

title: [Sortie] Exit-128 stuck-loop root-caused + fixed
body: three causes: (1) `after_create` didn't wipe the persistent per-issue workspace → `git clone` into a non-empty dir = exit 128 (`669d36b`); (2) hooks ran under a stripped env where `export …_proxy` didn't reach git/gh on the egress-internal network → pass proxy inline per command (`3f07151`); (3) single-file bind-mount inode trap meant `git pull` didn't update the in-container hook until `--force-recreate` (the `sortie-refresh` alias). #6 and #8 both ran clean afterward.

title: [Infra] HIGH — SQLite backup: consistent snapshots feeding Hyper Backup.
body: The NAS is backed up off-box (Synology Hyper Backup → Backblaze, versioned), which covers both `dashboard.db` and Sortie's `.sortie.db` (both live under `/volume1/docker/personal-dashboard/`). Gap: Hyper Backup does a file-level copy and is **not SQLite-aware** — with WAL on, a hot copy of `dashboard.db` + its `-wal`/`-shm` sidecars at slightly different instants can restore stale/inconsistent. Fix: a small cron using SQLite's online backup — `sqlite3 data/dashboard.db ".backup 'data/backups/dashboard.<UTC-stamp>.db'"` — which is **guaranteed-consistent even while the app runs**, then let Hyper Backup ship the snapshot. Reuse the pattern already in `ops/sortie/quota-refund.sh` (`.backup` + timestamped file + prune old copies by retention days). Also: (a) confirm the Hyper Backup source set actually includes `/volume1/docker/`, and (b) do one **test restore** to verify. (RAID = disk-failure tolerance, a separate axis; backup is the thing that matters and is largely handled.)

title: [Infra] HIGH — Reverse proxy for off-LAN access.
body: Set up a reverse proxy so the personal dashboard is reachable from outside the local network (e.g. on the phone off-wifi), per PROJECT.md §1's post-MVP goal. Options to weigh: Synology's built-in reverse proxy + DDNS + Let's Encrypt (simplest on this box), or Tailscale/Cloudflare Tunnel (no inbound port-forward, better security posture — aligns with the egress-hardening mindset). Leaning tailscale as its already being used for other apps running on the NAS. Must add **authentication** before exposing (the app is currently LAN-only, no auth — PROJECT.md §1). Mind the existing port map (8080=gluetun, 8088=prod app).

title: [Infra] Retry cap / max-attempts
body: ⚠️ PARTIALLY ADDRESSED (2026-06-30). The ~43× storm's root causes (exit-128 prep-retry loop) are fixed, and capped/parked issues are now made *visible* by `sortie-watchdog` (`sortie:stuck` + @mention) instead of silently grinding. Still open if wanted: a true per-issue max-attempts ceiling (Sortie's `max_sessions:3` caps agent sessions but not workspace-prep retries).

title: [Infra] Pro usage-limit handling
body: The retry storm exhausted the Claude Pro quota → instant `turn_failed`. Monitor usage and/or add an API-key fallback or guard (ties into RAM/CPU limits below).

title: Agent leaks `package.json` mutations into PRs — REVISIT ~2026-07-07
body: *(Steve, 2026-06-30)* — PR #7 carried out-of-scope manifest edits: a root `allowScripts` block + an `apps/web` `prepare` `|| true` regression. **Mechanism (now confirmed by elimination, in-container test):** it is **NOT** npm (plain `npm ci` in the container leaves the tree clean), **NOT** lavamoat (not installed, no postinstall hook), **NOT** repo/`.npmrc` config → it's the **Claude Code agent session's own install-script sandboxing** writing top-level `allowScripts` during the agent's work. It's **inert** in this repo (nothing reads `allowScripts`). **A structural `after_run` dep-manifest guard was built + Oracle-SHIP'd** (restore manifests to `origin/main` before staging, `.sortie/allow-dep-changes` sentinel opt-in) **then REVERTED from main** (`58bfa18`) per Steve — **preserved on branch `chore/dep-manifest-guard`** (was commit `f3d0007`), re-mergeable as-is. **Why parked, not shipped:** the clean "commit the `allowScripts` block to main once" fix assumed npm's idempotent `approve-scripts`, which collapsed when the test proved it's not npm; whether the *Claude Code* writer respects a pre-committed block is undocumented + untested, so committing is an unverified gamble and the guard's silent-revert was judged not worth keeping for an inert artifact while Steve still reviews every PR. **Revisit next week:** if manifest mutations keep appearing in Sortie PRs, re-investigate — options: (a) re-merge the guard from `chore/dep-manifest-guard`; (b) test the Claude Code writer's idempotency (run a `claude` session install with the block pre-committed, see if it rewrites); (c) deliberately *adopt* an install-script allowlist as repo policy (npm `approve-scripts` if container npm ≥ 11.16.0, or lavamoat) + a `git diff --exit-code` tripwire that fails loud instead of silent-reverting; (d) configure Claude Code not to gate install scripts. Real out-of-band enforcement belongs with the self-merge guardrails item above once supervision drops.

title: Harden guardrails into real boundaries before enabling agent self-merge
body: *(Steve, 2026-06-30; gated — ~months out)* — Today the actual authorization boundary on agent changes is **Steve manually reviewing + approving every bot PR** before merge (branch protection, bot can't self-merge). The in-sandbox controls (dep-manifest guard's `.sortie/allow-dep-changes` sentinel, prompt "don't touch X" rules) live in the agent's own workspace → **agent-self-grantable**, so they're **noise filters / review signal aids, NOT boundaries**. When moving to less-supervised merging (auto-merge on green, higher concurrency), the human gate disappears and these must become **out-of-band** controls the agent can't reach: e.g. a **GitHub Actions PR check** (runs in GitHub infra, outside the agent's container) that FAILS any PR touching dependency manifests / secrets / CI / forbidden paths unless a label only a human can apply (`sortie:allow-deps`) is present — the label is the auth token the agent can't set. Composes with the dep-guard (guard strips incidental noise pre-PR; CI hard-blocks deliberate changes pending the human label). Ties into: File access whitelist, `ask_human` enforcement, Context Monitor. **Revisit when self-merge / unsupervised autonomy is actually on the table.**

title: Build `ask_human` MCP tool (priority: near-term)
body: Custom MCP server the agent calls mid-session to ask a question. Posts to Discord/Dashboard, blocks until reply, returns the reply to the agent. Build alongside the initial Sortie deployment. **Enforcement mechanism (file-edit pre-hook vs read-only mount vs `chmod`-at-session-start vs prompt-only) is TBD at build/deploy time** — picked then, not now. See the "Guardrails — `ask_human` triggers" section below for the catalog of conditions that should call into this tool.

title: Issue-authoring for agents — enforce structure + a "brain-dump → ticket" skill
body: *(Steve, 2026-06-29)* — Two parts. **(a) Audit how/whether issue structure is *enforced*** for Sortie-picked issues. Today it's **prompt-only**: `WORKFLOW.md`'s template guides the agent and self-review checks `npm run verify`, but nothing validates the *issue* itself has acceptance criteria / scope before an agent burns a run on it. Decide if a structural control is warranted — e.g. a required GitHub issue template (`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`), a Sortie `dispatch` rule, or a pre-dispatch validator that bounces malformed issues. **(b) Build a template or skill** that turns Steve's stream-of-consciousness feature requests into the proper goal/acceptance-criteria/scope format fast. Investigate reusing existing skills (`to-issues`, `to-prd`, `triage`) and/or a GitHub issue template vs a new lightweight `/to-issues`-style skill. Goal: low-friction capture → well-formed ticket Sortie can act on without drift.

title: Verify the quota-refund janitor's DSM schedule fires unattended
body: *(Steve, 2026-06-30)* — The janitor (`ops/sortie/quota-refund.sh`, D-024 + idle-guard) works when run **manually** (full PATH), but the DSM Task Scheduler job's first run produced **no log file**, i.e. it never executed (likely the minimal-PATH gotcha — `docker`/`sqlite3` not on the scheduled task's PATH — or the schedule simply hadn't fired). Circle back: in DSM Task Scheduler hit **Run** → **View Result**; if `docker not found`, pin full paths (`/usr/local/bin/docker`) in the task or set `DOCKER_BIN`/`SQLITE_BIN` env in the command. Not "deployed" until an *unattended* run writes the expected log to `/volume1/docker/personal-dashboard/quota-refund.log`.

Sortie's adapter contract is documented; this is a deliberate "tracker-agnostic" extension point. Only worth doing if we want Linear's UI/views over GitHub Issues.

title: Synchronous — block until human approves:
body:
- *Scope:* modify any file outside `SORTIE_WORKSPACE` (the original case)
- *Secrets / credentials:* touch any file matching `.env*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa*`, `credentials*.json`, or a configured secret-file glob
- *Auth / authz code:* touch any path in a per-project "sensitive paths" list (auth middleware, permission models, session handling, CSP/CORS config)
- *Harness self-modification:* touch any of `SOUL.md`, `SOUL_*.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `SKILLS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, or files under `.claude/commands/`
- *Destructive git:* `git push --force`, history rewrite of pushed commits, branch delete, `reset --hard` against a remote-tracking branch
- *Direct-to-main:* push commits to `main`/`master` without a PR, or merge a PR without the review/CI feedback loop having completed
- *CI / deploy / infra config:* touch CI workflows, `Dockerfile`, deploy manifests, infra-as-code, or `package.json` scripts (especially `pre`/`post`-install — supply-chain vector)
- *Dependencies:* major-version bump (semver), add a dep from a publisher not already in `package.json`, pin to a git ref instead of a registry version
- *Database / schema:* create or modify migrations, run a migration, drop or alter a table or column
- *External effects:* outbound HTTP to a non-allowlisted domain; sending email / Slack / Discord on behalf of Steve; creating issues or PRs in a repo other than the assigned one
- *Bulk delete:* delete more than N files in a session (N configurable — start at 10)
- *Skip-the-bar:* `--no-verify` on commit/push, deleting tests to get a build green, or modifying tests in a way that reduces coverage of the changed code

title: Soft block — ask once when threshold crossed, then proceed:
body:
- Session token cost exceeds configured budget (e.g. $X per ticket)
- Session duration exceeds configured envelope (e.g. Y minutes)
- More than N files modified or M net lines changed in a single session (signal that scope has crept beyond the ticket)
- Add or remove a top-level directory or package

title: Async notify only (Discord ping after the fact, no block):
body:
- Self-Review failed and Sortie is about to retry
- Hit retry limit (session terminating in failure)
- New public API surface added (new exported function / route / endpoint)
- Modified existing public API signature (worth knowing — possible breaking change)
- Crossed a "soft" milestone like first commit, first push, first PR open

title: Decision (2026-05-27):
body:
- Mission Control UI lives in the **Personal Dashboard** project as a page, not as a standalone project. The Dashboard exists now; a separate project is overkill for one page calling one API.
- Orchestration runs on **Sortie** (single Go binary, SQLite persistence). Dashboard polls Sortie's `GET /api/v1/state` and `GET /api/v1/{identifier}` every 5–10s (no event stream available).
- Sortie address configured via `SORTIE_URL` env var in the Dashboard.

title: Limit:
body:
Sortie's HTTP API is read-only — no start/pause/kill actions, no human-answer endpoint. Mission Control is monitoring + drill-down only; intervention happens by editing tracker state (GitHub Issues) or by clicking through to the PR.
- Sortie's Dashboard is one thing, but we may still want further visibility into the entire harness system - Sortie + the inner loop. Agent-agent communication etc.
- This is also represented in a TODO inside the Personal Dashboard project, but keeping a reference here as well for now.

title: Auto-PR creation
body: Agent commits and pushes; Sortie's `after_run` hook stages, commits, and pushes the branch.

title: Retry with backoff
body: Sortie state machine handles retry queue, stall detection, timeout enforcement.

The broader harness-level Oracle/QA pattern (grading test-skill results, dispatch outputs, etc.) is **not** covered.

title: PR-reaction feedback loop
body: Sortie watches reactions on the PR and feeds them into a follow-up attempt.

body:
Once the personal dashboard is up and running with a few widgets and the harness is stable, look through both `personal-dashboard` and `core` projects for references to the same file paths. Bump those paths out to a master config that is imported by both projects to prevent projects from getting out of sync with each other. Create a skill that scans **all* projects in the projects folder doing a similar check. Manual run is fine for now but add a TODO for having that skill run weekly, or at least a weekly reminder / notification for me to run the skill manually.

title: [Agent Dashboard] Phase 3 — Convert ticket to Sortie issue
body:
Per-ticket "Convert to Sortie issue": Claude API formats the ticket into the Sortie issue format (goal/acceptance-criteria/scope) → draft-then-approve → creates a sortie:queued GitHub issue in the project's repo → links issue back to the ticket. Needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + write-scoped GH token on the NAS. Only for sortie_enabled projects.

--- Added 2026-07-01 (queued-status polling automation) ---
Poll for tickets in the Queued status; when one is found that has no associated GitHub issue, create a GitHub issue for it and tag it `sortie:queued`. Ticket cards in Queued (and any status after it) show a link to the GitHub issue — just the GitHub icon.

title: [Agent Dashboard] Derived status sync from GitHub labels
body:
node-cron job polls GitHub for each converted ticket's sortie:* label + PR merge-state and writes in_progress / in_review / completed onto the row (the derived columns). Avoids coupling to Sortie's internal :7678 API.

When a ticket is being actively worked on by an agent, a subtle shimmer effect should be applied to the card.

title: Fetch Core TODOs from the dashboard ticket API instead of META-TODOS.md
body: META-TODOS.md stays canonical/Markdown for now (it's load-bearing — referenced across core's README/PROJECT/DECISIONS/MEMORY, and PROJECT.md §42 mandates Markdown). But core's tickets now also live in the dashboard board. Migrate core's references (session-start, /project-maintenance, /project-status) to read Core's tickets from the Agent Dashboard ticket API (GET /api/widgets/agent-dashboard/tickets filtered to project=core) so there's one source of truth. Requires the dashboard API reachable from core sessions + a sync/authoring path back. Ties into the META-TODOS 'memory-library'/'replicate Core dev tooling' items.

title: Widget dynamic grid system
body:
Widgets should live in an invisible grid system, and be resizeable, snapping to invisible grid markers, similar to how Datadog Dashboards work. Grid should be 8 units wide at large screen sizes. Each unit is a square.

Grid markers become visible when re-sizing.

title: [Infra] Version-control the global generic skills (wrap-up, project-maintenance)
body:
The global generic base skills live only at ~/.claude/commands/ (project-maintenance.md, wrap-up.md) and are NOT under any git repo. The memory-bloat sensor added in D-025 (index ≤200-char cap, 12KB hot day-file, 32KB load budget) therefore exists only on this machine's disk — a fresh machine would not have it, and there is no history/backup.

core-session-start is already safe: its global copy is a symlink to the canonical repo file (core/.claude/commands/core-session-start.md). The generic base skills need the same treatment.

Proposed fix: bring the generic base skills into the core repo (e.g. core/.claude/commands/global/) and symlink them into ~/.claude/commands/, so both the generic base and the Core delegate-then-extend copies live in git. Decide the in-repo layout first.

Ref: DECISIONS.md D-025.

body: Backlog tickets get a "Refine" button that launches an agent chat in a modal, instructed to refine the work via the `/grill-me` skill, followed by a `/to-issues` skill. End result: tickets in the Ready column are formatted appropriately for Sortie agents. One backlog ticket may yield more than one Ready ticket. Involves determining the exact prompt structure — use the existing structure + the prompt structure defined for harness subagents as a template.

body: Verify the evaluator layer is actually engaged in the Sortie pipeline (generation ≠ evaluation) and not silently bypassed.

body: Get notified via Discord when a PR is open and ready to merge, or when commits are pushed to a PR where I requested changes. Related to the broader PD-6 [Sortie] Discord Integration.

title: [Mission Control] Warn when a ticket moved into Queued is not properly formatted
body: When a ticket is moved into the Queued status, issue a warning if it is not formatted properly (i.e. not in the Sortie-ready shape produced by the Refine flow, see PD-172 / PD-164).

title: [Ticket Board] Icon denoting a ticket is being worked on by a sortie agent
body: All in progress, in review, and completed tickets may have been completed by a human or an agent. Any ticket that is associated with a github issue tagged sortie:queued or sortie:in-progress should have its assignee field set to 'agent' and any ticket assigned to an agent should have a robot icon on its card.

title: [Infra] Ticket Audit Cron Job
body: Every 24 or 48 hours, run a job that launches an agent to review the backlog and ready tickets for each project, cross-referencing them with the project code, memory and decisiosn docs. Determine if any ticket has been superceded, is no longer worth doing, or if priority has changed. Produce an interactive report showing what tickets were flagged, grouped by project and then by priority, with a button to accept the recommendation, reject it, or do something else.
